User.title: User
User.description: Represents a participant in the AlphaBet League.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: The user's chosen display name or handle within the application.

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user has administrative privileges (e.g., to hide/show scores).

LeagueConfiguration.title: LeagueConfiguration
LeagueConfiguration.description: Stores global settings and configurable values for the AlphaBet League. This is expected to be a singleton entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LeagueConfiguration entity (e.g., 'global_config').

defaultRoundValue.description: The default point value assigned to a round for saldo calculation, if no specific value is set.

roundSpecificValues.description: An array of objects, each specifying a custom point value for a particular round number, overriding the default.

Round.title: Round
Round.description: Represents a specific betting 'rodada' or matchday in the league.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Round entity (e.g., 'round_1', 'rodada_2026_01').

roundNumber.description: The sequential number of the round in the league calendar.

name.description: The descriptive name of the round (e.g., 'Rodada 1', 'Rodada Especial de Férias').

isScoresHidden.description: Indicates whether player betting scores for this round are currently hidden from other users by an administrator.

winningPlayerIds.description: References to the User entities who are declared winners for this specific round. (Relationship: User N:N Round)

dateCreated.description: The date and time when this round was initially created or recorded.

dateUpdated.description: The date and time when the round's data was last modified.

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Details about a single football game that is part of a round.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Match entity.

externalApiId.description: The identifier for this match from the external sports data API, used for synchronization.

roundId.description: Reference to the Round this match belongs to. (Relationship: Round 1:N Match)

homeTeamName.description: The full name of the home team participating in the match.

awayTeamName.description: The full name of the away team participating in the match.

homeTeamAbbreviation.description: The abbreviated name of the home team (e.g., 'PAL').

awayTeamAbbreviation.description: The abbreviated name of the away team (e.g., 'BOT').

homeTeamCrestUrl.description: URL to the crest (logo) image of the home team.

awayTeamCrestUrl.description: URL to the crest (logo) image of the away team.

utcDate.description: The UTC date and time when the match is scheduled or was played.

status.description: The current status of the match (e.g., 'SCHEDULED', 'IN_PLAY', 'FINISHED').

fullTimeHomeScore.description: The final score for the home team after full time. Null if the match is not finished.

fullTimeAwayScore.description: The final score for the away team after full time. Null if the match is not finished.

PlayerBet.title: PlayerBet
PlayerBet.description: A specific score prediction made by a user for a particular match.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerBet entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this bet. (Relationship: User 1:N PlayerBet)

matchId.description: Reference to the Match for which this bet was made. (Relationship: Match 1:N PlayerBet)

homeScorePrediction.description: The user's predicted score for the home team.

awayScorePrediction.description: The user's predicted score for the away team.

dateSubmitted.description: The date and time when the bet was submitted or last updated.

RoundPlayerScore.title: RoundPlayerScore
RoundPlayerScore.description: Stores a player's calculated performance and points for a specific round.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RoundPlayerScore entity.

roundId.description: Reference to the Round this score belongs to. (Relationship: Round 1:N RoundPlayerScore)

userId.description: Reference to the User whose score this is. (Relationship: User 1:N RoundPlayerScore)

totalPoints.description: The total points accumulated by the player in this specific round.

exactScoreCount.description: The number of exact score predictions made by the player in this round.

PlayerOverallStats.title: PlayerOverallStats
PlayerOverallStats.description: Stores a player's cumulative statistics across all completed rounds in the league.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerOverallStats entity (e.g., 'user_id_stats').

userId.description: Reference to the User whose overall statistics these are. (Relationship: User 1:1 PlayerOverallStats)

totalVictories.description: The total number of rounds where the player was a sole winner.

totalDraws.description: The total number of rounds where the player tied with others for the win.

currentBalance.description: The player's current cumulative balance (saldo) based on round values and wins/losses.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the player's overall statistics were last calculated or updated.

definition.description: User profiles, accessible by the user themselves or by administrators. Each document's ID corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Global administrators. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{userId}` grants administrative privileges to the associated user ID. This collection uses existence for DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user with administrative privileges.

definition.description: Singleton document storing global league configurations. Read by all, writeable only by administrators.
Information about each betting round, including its name, number, and whether scores are currently hidden. Writeable by administrators, readable by all authenticated users.

params.name: roundId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific betting round.

definition.description: Details for individual matches within a specific round. Includes denormalized `roundId` for direct querying and to link to the parent round. Readable by all authenticated users.

params.name: roundId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent round.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific match.

definition.description: Individual score predictions made by a user for a match. Includes denormalized `roundId` and `isScoresHidden` (from the parent Round) for authorization independence, allowing atomic writes and reads without `get()` calls in security rules. Accessible by the owner, or by all if `isScoresHidden` is false.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who made the bet.

params.name: betId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific player bet.

definition.description: Calculated scores and points for each player within a specific round. Includes denormalized `isScoresHidden` (from the parent Round) for authorization independence, allowing atomic writes and reads without `get()` calls in security rules. Accessible by the owner, or by all if `isScoresHidden` is false.

params.name: roundId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent round.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user whose score this is.

definition.description: Cumulative statistics for a specific player across all rounds. This is a singleton document per user. Accessible by the owner or administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user whose overall statistics these are.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for AlphaBet League prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access control by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The hierarchy is primarily organized around `rounds` for global event data and `users` for individual user-specific data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This is achieved through strategic denormalization. For `PlayerBet` and `RoundPlayerScore` entities, which are sensitive to the 'isScoresHidden' status of their parent `Round`, the `isScoresHidden` field from the `Round` document is directly copied into each `PlayerBet` and `RoundPlayerScore` document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check the parent `Round`'s status, thereby preserving atomic operations (transactions/batches) and making rule evaluation more efficient and robust. For example, a `PlayerBet` document at `/users/{userId}/bets/{betId}` will contain `isScoresHidden: true/false`, allowing rules to grant read access if `request.auth.uid == userId` OR `resource.data.isScoresHidden == false` (for other users).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure supports Query-Able Paths (QAPs) through a combination of structural segregation and the aforementioned denormalization:
1.  **Structural Segregation:** Publicly accessible data (e.g., `Rounds`, `Matches`) are placed in top-level collections (`/rounds/{roundId}`). Private, user-specific data (e.g., `PlayerBet`, `PlayerOverallStats`) is housed under user-scoped collections (`/users/{userId}/bets`). This segregation naturally restricts `list` operations to data with a homogeneous security posture.
2.  **Denormalization for `isScoresHidden`:** For collections where visibility is dynamic (`/users/{userId}/bets` and `/rounds/{roundId}/player_scores`), the denormalized `isScoresHidden` field directly within each document enables secure `list` operations. Users can query these collections, and the security rules can evaluate access for each document based on its internal `isScoresHidden` field, preventing unauthorized reads of individual documents while allowing the query itself to succeed. This means a non-admin/non-owner user can query for `player_scores` for a `round` and only receive documents where `isScoresHidden` is false, without the rule attempting to filter results on the server side.

**DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** Global roles, such as administrators, are managed using dedicated collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{userId}`). The presence of a document at this path signifies an administrative role for the corresponding `userId`, allowing security rules to perform a simple `exists()` check.

**Overall, the design:**
*   Uses clear, descriptive paths (`{userId}`, `{roundId}`, `{matchId}`).
*   Employs hierarchical paths for 1:N relationships (e.g., `Round` to `Match`, `User` to `PlayerBet`).
*   Ensures `ownerId` (via path or explicit field) and timestamps (`dateCreated`, `dateUpdated`) are consistently applied.
*   Explicitly models state with fields like `status` in `Match` for clarity.
